task: Craft Twitter/X strategy for askr launch: determine tweet positioning, identify high-reach accounts to follow, and build authentic engagement path without premature product reveal
discussion_summary: User has tweeted about problems askr solves but not the solution yet. Rejected weak positioning ('been building the fix') as it undersells askr's scope. Clarified askr isn't ready for public launch (1 week out). Needs honest reach strategy: follow relevant builders/influencers, engage authentically on their threads rather than self-promote, and position as knowledgeable voice in the space before product reveal.

in_progress.what: Removed Phase 4 Public Launch section (deleted lines 294-309) — likely to restructure launch timeline post-session

next_actions.action: Curate follow list: identify 15-20 accounts (@lachygroom, @swyx, @levelsio, @marc_louvion style) who build in AI/dev tooling space and already have engaged audiences. Prioritize those posting about Claude, context limits, workflow automation.
next_actions.why: Reach comes from authentic engagement in existing communities, not self-promotion. These accounts will naturally align with askr's value prop.

next_actions.action: Draft engagement strategy: for next 1 week until launch, reply to 2-3 threads daily from followed accounts with genuine insights about Claude context management, session persistence, or workflow problems. Never mention askr yet.
next_actions.why: Builds credibility and audience before product reveal. User becomes 'the person who knows this stuff' rather than another product launcher.

next_actions.action: Rewrite next tweet to hint at broader askr scope without revealing solution. Example: 'claude context limits aren't the only problem. been building something that fixes the whole workflow.' Keep vague, keep momentum.
next_actions.why: Current tweet ('been building the fix') undersells askr. User rejected it because askr does more than just the two problems mentioned. New version maintains arc without overpromising.

next_actions.action: Finalize Phase 4 Public Launch section in roadmap.md with realistic 1-week timeline. Include: GIF/screenshot assets, clean install story, launch thread outline, brew tap setup.
next_actions.why: Roadmap was partially deleted this session. Needs reconstruction with actual launch date to unblock final week of development.

next_actions.action: Do NOT post solution-focused tweets until public launch. Keep narrative as 'building something' until GitHub release is live.
next_actions.why: User explicitly rejected premature reveal. Maintains scarcity and launch impact.

decisions.decision: Do not post detailed solution tweet yet; keep positioning vague until 1-week launch window
decisions.reason: askr not ready for public use. Premature reveal kills launch momentum and credibility.

decisions.decision: Prioritize authentic engagement over direct promotion for reach building
decisions.reason: User has zero time for traditional marketing. Engagement in existing communities is only scalable path.

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Tweet: 'been building the fix for both. one week out.'
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: The tweet you gave makes askr look weak, as that's not the only two things askr is [building]

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Post solution details now to close narrative arc
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: I don't want to post a solution, cause askr isn't built yet, I think I am still a week out from public launch

failed_approaches.approach: Generic reach advice (reply to threads, post value) without specific account targets
failed_approaches.reason: User asked for concrete list of people to follow. Generic strategy insufficient without actionable targets.

failed_approaches.approach: Weak positioning tweet that only references the two problems mentioned in previous tweets
failed_approaches.reason: Undersells askr's actual scope and capabilities. User rejected as making product look limited.

relational_files.why: User asked to verify askr's actual capabilities before crafting messaging. README is source of truth for feature scope.

blockers: No curated list of target accounts yet — needed to start engagement strategy immediately
Exact scope of askr features unclear from session — need to review README/docs to craft positioning that doesn't undersell
